situational_context: Early structured rebuild — Week 2 of return-to-running protocol. Run #2 completed today (2.01 km, 18 min, avg HR 144, max HR 174, suffer 15). HR response elevated vs Run #1 (avg 135, max 148, suffer 7) despite slower pace — likely fewer walk breaks and possible residual fatigue from yesterday's gravel. Chronic load at 22.7 (1.4% of historical peak 1608). Weight trending down: 106.9→104.1 kg in 3 weeks (~0.93 kg/week). RHR improved 53→49 bpm. ACWR 0.67 — below optimal band (0.8-1.3), acute load dipped as big gravel sessions rotated out of 7-day window. Two 'sur site' days ahead (Mon-Tue) will contribute commute load. Baby Léonie (4 months) — sleep adequate.

plan_7d_ahead.type: Run #3 conditionnel OU Endurance vélo Z2

recommendations.text: Monitor Run #2 tissue feedback over next 24-48h. Elevated HR (144 avg vs 135 on Run #1) at slower pace needs watching. If any localized pain (Achilles, patellar, tibia, plantar) persists >24h, skip Run #3 Friday and substitute cycling
recommendations.measurable: Zero localized pain >24h post-Run #2; if clear, proceed to Run #3 on May 15

recommendations.text: Increase protein intake toward 150g/day target. Current 7-day average is 124g (83% of target). At 104 kg with active rebuild, 1.4-1.6 g/kg/day supports tendon and muscle adaptation
recommendations.measurable: Average protein ≥140g/day over the next 7 days

recommendations.text: Rebuild acute load toward ACWR 0.8+ by maintaining consistent daily activity. Current ACWR 0.67 is below optimal band. Sunday cycling + commute rides Mon-Tue + Wed/Thu sessions should restore the ratio naturally

markers_to_watch.threshold: Run #3 avg HR should be ≤145 bpm at similar pace/distance to Run #2
markers_to_watch.reason: Run #2 showed elevated HR (144 avg) vs Run #1 (135 avg) at slower pace. If Run #3 shows further elevation, cardiovascular adaptation to running impact is lagging
Currently 0.67 — below optimal. Big gravel sessions rotating out of 7-day window caused the drop. Consistent training should restore it

markers_to_watch.threshold: Maintain downward trend, not >1 kg/week loss
markers_to_watch.reason: Losing at ~0.93 kg/week — right at the edge. Faster loss compromises tendon adaptation during return to running
